LAW AND MOTION, 302, BOERSCH SHAPIRO LLP'S MOTION TO WITHDRAW AS COUNSEL IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ALTHOUGH NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED, THE MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1362. CRC 3.1362(A) PROVIDES THAT A MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL MUST BE MADE ON JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM MC-051. ADDITIONALLY, CRC 3.1362(E) PROVIDES THAT A PROPOSED ORDER MUST BE PREPARED ON JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM MC-053 AND MUST BE LODGED WITH THE COURT WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THESE REQUIREMENTS. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jul-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. Ruling: The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DAVID W. SHAPIRO'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR AMERICAN OPPORTUNITY PARTNERS, LLC AND 1102 TARAVAL STREET, LLC is granted. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Jul-02-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. Ruling: The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS DAMIEN QUINN, AMERICAN OPPORTUNITY PARTNERS, LLC, AND 1102 TARAVAL STREET, LLC'S Motion For Relief From Default is denied. The evidence submitted by Defendants does not overcome the presumption of correctness set forth in Evid. Code sec. 647. (See Rodriguez v. Nam Min Cho (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 742, 750, "Evidence Code section 647 provides that a registered process server's declaration of service establishes a presumption that the facts stated in the declaration are true.") Further, the Court finds the Declaration of Javier Baraona about the service to be more credible than the Declaration of Damien Quinn. Mr. Baraona attaches contemporaneous notes that establish that Mr. Baraona got close enough to Mr. Quinn to describe his height, weight, hair color, and eye color. (Baraona Decl., Ex. A.) Mr. Baraona also declares that it is his usual practice to take notes about any unusual personal service, but he took no such notes here. (Baraona Decl., pars. 16-17.) In any event, case law holds that substantial compliance with the service requirements is sufficient to defeat a motion brought under CCP 473(d). (Gibble v. Car-Lene Research, Inc. (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 295, 313, "It is well settled that strict compliance with statutes governing service of process is not required. Rather, in deciding whether service was valid, the statutory provisions regarding service of process should be literally construed to effectuate service and uphold the jurisdiction of the court if actual notice has been received by the defendant;" see also Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544; Rutter Group, Civ. Proc. Before Trial, 5:485.) Additionally, there is insufficient evidence of mistake or excusable neglect. The Declaration of Steven Lau establishes that Mr. Quinn was granted two one-week extensions to file the answer, but did not do so. If Mr. Lau had told Mr. Quinn he would re-serve the complaint because service was defective, as contended by Mr. Quinn, there would have been no need for an extension of the time to answer. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Boersch Shapiro Llp'S Motion To Withdraw As Counsel (Control Date)
Off Calendar Jun-18-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Boersch Shapiro LLP'S Motion To Withdraw As Counsel is denied without prejudice. Although no opposition was filed, the motion does not comply with CRC 3.1362. CRC 3.1362(a) provides that a motion to be relieved as counsel MUST be made on Judicial Council form MC-051. Additionally, CRC 3.1362(e) provides that a proposed order MUST be prepared on Judicial Council form MC-053 and MUST be lodged with the court with the moving papers. Moving party failed to comply with these requirements. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
